articles.title: Bengaluru sees rising heat; Hailstorm alert issued in parts of Karnataka - NewsFirst Prime
articles.full_text:
Temperatures rise across Bengaluru and multiple districts, while IMD warns of heatwave in coastal areas and hailstorms in north and south interior Karnataka
Karnataka is witnessing a sharp rise in temperatures along with sudden weather changes, as the state faces both intense heatwave conditions and the possibility of hailstorms in several regions. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued alerts, warning that the situation could worsen over the next two days.
Bengaluru and many other parts of the state are already experiencing a noticeable increase in temperature. At the same time, coastal and Kalyana Karnataka regions are struggling under severe heat conditions. According to forecasts, temperatures in these areas could rise up to 45 degrees Celsius in the coming days, adding to public discomfort.
Authorities have advised people to avoid unnecessary outdoor activities, especially between 10 am and 5 pm, when the heat is expected to be at its peak.

In coastal Karnataka, including Uttara Kannada, Udupi, and Dakshina Kannada districts, heatwave conditions are likely to continue, and official heatwave alerts have been issued for these regions.
Meanwhile, parts of north interior Karnataka are expected to see a different weather pattern. Districts such as Bagalkote, Belagavi, Kalaburagi, and Vijayapura are likely to experience thunderstorms accompanied by hailstorms.
Similar weather activity is also expected in south interior Karnataka. The IMD has forecast thunderstorms with lightning and hailstorms in districts including Chikkamagaluru, Hassan, Kodagu, and Shivamogga.
Apart from these regions, the rest of the state is expected to witness mostly dry weather conditions.

The combination of extreme heat and sudden storm activity has raised concerns, with authorities urging people to stay cautious and follow weather advisories closely over the next few days.
